data.title: Netflix Stock Slides after Q4 Earnings as Investors Weigh What Comes Next
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) stock continues to slide even after the company reported better-than-expected Q4 results, as investors focused on Netflix's proposed all-cash deal for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and its broader strategy rather than the quarter itself. The reaction suggests the market is looking past near-term performance and weighing what comes next for the streaming giant.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

To add context, Netflix posted fourth-quarter revenue of $12.1 billion, modestly above Wall Street expectations of $11.97 billion, while adjusted earnings came in at $0.56 per share, also slightly ahead of forecasts of $0.55 per share. Subscriber growth remained strong, with paid memberships climbing to 325 million, reinforcing Netflix's position as the largest paid streaming platform globally.

Despite those positives, the market reaction was cautious.

Investors Look Beyond Recent Results

Investors appeared less focused on Netflix's quarterly results and more on what the company plans to do next. Netflix said it will keep spending heavily on content and new projects, which could weigh on margins in the short term, even if it supports growth over time.

For 2026, Netflix expects revenue between $50.7 billion and $51.7 billion. While that still points to solid growth, the lower end of the range came in slightly below expectations, adding to the cautious reaction.

Ads and Live Content Remain Key Growth Drivers

Advertising is becoming a more important part of Netflix's business. The company said ad revenue more than doubled last year and is expected to roughly double again in 2026 to about $3 billion. Ads give Netflix more ways to make money, but growing this business also brings higher costs.

Netflix is also expanding live content, including sports and events outside the U.S., as it looks to keep viewers engaged beyond traditional shows and movies. To support this effort, the company is building new operations centers in international markets.

The Warner Bros. Deal Raises New Questions

Investors are also watching Netflix's proposed all-cash deal for Warner Bros. closely. Netflix said it would pause share buybacks to save cash and has secured large bridge loans to help fund the deal.

Management believes the acquisition would add a large and valuable content library and support long-term growth. Still, the size of the deal, along with execution and regulatory risks, has shifted investor focus away from short-term results and toward how well Netflix can manage costs and balance sheet pressure.

Is NFLX Stock a Good Buy Now? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 26 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NFLX price target of $124.80 per share implies 43% upside potential. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following the earnings report.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Are PAVmed (PAVM) & Lucid Diagnostics (LUCD) Stocks Up Today?
data.description:
PAVmed (PAVM) stock rocketed higher on Wednesday after the medical technology company announced a major contract for its subsidiary, Lucid Diagnostics (LUCD). The company revealed that Lucid Diagnostics has been awarded a contract by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) for its EsoGuard Esophageal DNA Test.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

PAVmed noted that this contract expands precancer testing to the more than nine million enrolled veterans annually in VA health insurance. The contract has a pre-negotiated set price for EsoGuard that matches the established Medicare payment rate determined by the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS).

Shaun O'Neil, President and Chief Operating Officer of Lucid Diagnostics, said, "We are proud to partner with the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs to expand access to EsoGuard for our nation's veterans. The VA operates at significant clinical and operational scale, and selection by a healthcare system of this size reflects the strength of the clinical evidence supporting EsoGuard. We look forward to working with the VA and additional integrated health systems around the country to incorporate EsoGuard into standard clinical practice."

PAVmed & Lucid Diagnostics Stock Movements Today

PAVmed stock was up 134.89% in pre-market trading on Wednesday, following a 5.93% drop yesterday. The shares have slipped 6.5% year-to-date and 65.03% over the past 12 months. Today's news brought with it heavy trading, as some 8 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 377,000 units.

Lucid Diagnostics stock was up 9.92% in pre-market trading on Wednesday. The stock has rallied 11.01% year-to-date and 46.84% over the past 12 months. The shares experienced heavy trading today, as some 1.5 million units changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average of about 594,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: "Agonizingly Slow at First," Elon Musk Warns as Tesla Stock Faces Cybercab, Optimus Reality Check
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk said early production of the company's Cybercab robotaxi and its humanoid robot, Optimus, will move at an "agonizingly slow" pace before picking up speed later, Reuters reported.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musk's comments came in response to a post on X suggesting Cybercab production could begin in less than 100 days. While that timeline caught investor attention, Musk quickly tempered expectations, saying the ramp depends heavily on complexity.

"For Cybercab and Optimus, almost everything is new," Musk wrote. "So the early production rate will be agonizingly slow, but eventually end up being insanely fast."

A Big Bet with a Long Runway

The update is important because much of Tesla's roughly $1.39 trillion valuation is based on future bets like self-driving technology and humanoid robots, not on current electric vehicle sales, which still bring in most of the company's revenue and profit.

The Cybercab is a fully autonomous, two-seat vehicle with no steering wheel or pedals, showing how far Tesla is moving beyond traditional cars. The company plans to begin volume production of the Cybercab in 2026, with Optimus production expected to start toward the end of that year.

Early Testing Is Underway, but Scale Will Take Time

Tesla has already taken some early steps. In December, Musk said the company was testing robotaxis without safety monitors in the front passenger seat. Last year, Tesla also launched a limited robotaxi service in Austin, Texas, using Model Y vehicles equipped with its Full Self-Driving software. These early tests were limited to specific areas and still included human oversight.

Even so, scaling production remains a major challenge, and Musk's comments suggest investors should expect a slow build rather than quick results.

Is Tesla Stock Worth Buying?

Analysts remain sidelined on Tesla. On TipRanks, Tesla shares have a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $397.47 implies 5.19% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA stock has gained 1.14%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Rigetti Stock Is Up Today — and Where This Analyst Sees It Going Next
data.description:
Rigetti Computing (RGTI) shares were higher in premarket trading after the company announced an $8.4 million order for a 108-qubit quantum computer, a sign of growing interest in owning quantum systems rather than relying only on cloud access.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That news quickly drew a response from Wall Street.

Wedbush Securities analyst Antoine Legault kept his Outperform rating on Rigetti and raised his price target to $40 from $35, saying the order shows real demand is starting to build.

Where the Analyst Sees Rigetti Heading Next

Legault said this is not the first sign of growing customer interest in Rigetti's systems. Just four months ago, Rigetti announced $5.7 million in orders for two smaller quantum systems to be delivered in early 2026, showing demand is already building.

He believes these deals reflect growing interest from governments and research groups in on-site quantum computers, used alongside traditional systems. As the technology improves, he expects commercial customers to move in the same direction.

Because quantum computing is still early and long-term leaders are not yet clear, Legault said actual purchase orders matter more than announcements. In his view, these orders suggest that technically skilled buyers are backing Rigetti's superconducting approach.

Looking ahead, he expects more customers to place similar orders as Rigetti continues to scale its systems. He pointed to rising qubit counts and better performance as key drivers, especially as system accuracy moves closer to 99.9%.

Is RGTI a Good Stock to Buy? 

On the Street, Rigetti holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating, based on 11 analysts' ratings. The average RGTI stock price is $39.78, implying a 59.17% upside from the current price.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall after Tuesday's Steep Selloff
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down on Wednesday morning after the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) recorded their largest single-day drops since October 10 in the previous trading session. Traders reacted to U.S. President Donald Trump's threat to escalate tariffs over his demand to acquire Greenland. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500, and the Dow Jones were down 0.10%, 0.02%, and 0.20%, respectively, at 8:21 a.m. EST on January 21.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading session, the three major indexes closed sharply lower, with technology stocks leading the selloff. The Dow Jones and the S&P 500 declined by 1.76% and 2.06%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite fell 2.39%. Looking forward, traders are focused on Trump's speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos today, where he will discuss the Greenland issue.

Turning to trending stocks, Netflix (NFLX) shares fell more than 7% in pre-market trading, despite the streaming giant beating sales and earnings expectations for Q4 2025 and reporting a solid surge in global paid subscribers to 325 million. Investors seemed to be concerned about the impact of rising costs on margins. Meanwhile, NFLX revised its bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) to an all-cash offer, fending off competition from Paramount Skydance (PSKY).

Meanwhile, United Airlines (UAL) stock rose more than 3% after the airline said that it expects to generate record earnings in 2026, driven by solid travel demand. In contrast, Kraft Heinz (KHC) stock fell more than 5% after Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) registered its entire 27.5% stake in the consumer goods company for potential sale.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: PLD Earnings: Prologis Stock Jumps on Q4 Beats
data.description:
Prologis (PLD) stock was up on Wednesday alongside the release of the logistics real estate company's Q4 2025 earnings report. This reported started with adjusted earnings per share of $1.44, which was better than Wall Street's estimate of 70 cents. However, the company's adjusted EPS did slip 4% from the $1.50 reported in Q4 2024.

data.title: Google (GOOGL) Sees AI Glasses Replacing Smartphones Over Time
data.description:
Google (GOOGL) DeepMind Chief Executive Demis Hassabis shared his view on AI at the World Economic Forum in Davos on January 20. He said smart glasses are likely to replace phones as the main way people use AI over time. According to Hassabis, phones work today, but they are not ideal for daily AI help. Instead, glasses allow hands-free use while cooking, walking, or asking for help.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I'm not sure that glasses will be the final form, but it's definitely an outstanding form," Hassabis said.

In addition, he said early smart glasses failed due to weak batteries and a lack of clear use. Now, he believes AI tools like Gemini can fill that gap. Google's parent, Alphabet, has already announced plans to launch AI glasses in 2026 with partners like Samsung (SSNLF) and Warby Parker (WRBY).

AI Progress and the Road Ahead

At the same time, Hassabis said AI progress is moving far faster than past tech shifts. He compared the pace to the Industrial Revolution but said today's change is much faster. Looking ahead, he kept his view that there is a 50% chance AI reaches human-level skills by 2030. However, he added that key work is still needed in memory and long-term thinking.

Meanwhile, Hassabis said China has closed much of the AI gap with the West. Even so, he said Chinese firms are better at catching up than leading new ideas. "They're very good at catching up to where the frontier is," he said.

Finally, Hassabis confirmed Google has no plans to add ads to Gemini. That stance sets it apart from other AI tools that are testing paid content.

We used TipRanks' comparison Tool to align all the tickers mentioned in the piece, alongside Meta Platforms (META) and Vuzix (VUZI), which already work on AR glasses, and Apple (AAPL), which is also rumored to be working on in-house AI-enabled smart glasses.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings. Here Is What to Expect
data.description:
Medical technology company Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on January 22. ISRG is known for its da Vinci minimally invasive surgical systems. On January 14, the company announced its preliminary Q4 2025 results ahead of its presentation at the 44th Annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference. It reported a 19% year-over-year growth in Q4 2025 revenue to $2.87 billion.

However, ISRG stock fell as the company's outlook for global da Vinci procedure growth of 13% to 15% for 2026 fell short of expectations.

Meanwhile, Wall Street expects Intuitive Surgical to report a 2% year-over-year growth in Q4 2025 earnings per share (EPS) to $2.25.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/21/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rise after S&P 500, Dow's Largest Drop Since October
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher early Wednesday after the S&P 500 and the 30-stock Dow recorded their largest single-day drops since October 10. Traders are reacting to President Donald Trump's threat to escalate tariffs over his demand to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.33%, 0.34%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 3:20 a.m. EST on January 21.

During Tuesday's regular trading session, the three major indexes closed sharply lower, with technology stocks leading the selloff. The Dow declined by 1.76%, the S&P 500 fell by 2.06%, and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 2.39%.

After the markets closed, Netflix (NFLX) stock fell more than 5%, despite beating sales and earnings expectations for Q4FY25 and reporting a solid surge in global paid subscribers to 325 million. Moreover, the streaming giant revised its bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) to an all-cash offer, fending off competition from Paramount Skydance (PSKY).

Looking forward, traders are focused on Trump's speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos today, where he will discuss the Greenland issue. Additionally, economic reports on housing starts, MBA mortgage applications, building permits, and pending home sales will be released today.

On the earnings front, companies reporting today include Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Halliburton (HAL), and Charles Schwab (SCHW).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.26%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.63 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,855 per ounce on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower today as traders anticipate Trump's speech in front of world leaders at Davos.

Asia-Pacific Markets Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on January 21 amid escalated geopolitical tensions involving Iran and Greenland.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.36%. In China, the Shanghai Composite slipped 0.08%, while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.80%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei dropped 0.41%, and the Topix declined 0.99%.
